parts.text:
"Friends, fellow citizens, and stakeholders in our national future:

We are standing at a crossroads. For too long, we have treated the most sacred duty of citizenship—the selection of our leaders and the steering of our legislative course—as a participation trophy. We have laboured under the delusion that every opinion carries equal weight, regardless of the capacity to understand the complex consequences of those opinions. 

Let us speak plainly about the world we live in. We are navigating a century defined by quantum computing, intricate geopolitical maneuvers, high-stakes economics, and technical challenges that were unimaginable to our ancestors. To govern effectively, a society requires a baseline of cognitive competence. Why do we require a license to drive a car? Why do we require certifications to practice medicine or engineering? Because we recognize that incompetence in those fields leads to catastrophe. Yet, we allow the most complex 'machinery' of all—the State—to be operated by those who struggle to grasp basic logic or long-term causality.

This policy isn't about exclusion for its own sake; it is about **stewardship**. 

A voter who cannot comprehend the mechanics of inflation, the nuances of constitutional law, or the difference between rhetoric and reality is a voter who is easily manipulated by demagogues. When we allow the cognitively vulnerable to be weaponized by populist slogans, we aren't practicing 'democracy'—we are practicing organized chaos. We are essentially allowing the least capable among us to cancel out the votes of those who have spent their lives studying the issues.

By implementing a cognitive threshold, we ensure that the electorate is composed of individuals who can analyze data, foresee consequences, and resist the emotional manipulation that currently plagues our political discourse. We are raising the bar for our civilization. We are ensuring that the pilot of the ship actually understands the navigation charts.

Critics will call this 'elitism.' I call it **rationalism**. If we want a first-class nation, we cannot be governed by second-class logic. It is time to treat the ballot with the same rigor we treat every other essential function of a modern, functioning society. It is time to ensure that the future of our children is decided by those with the mental faculty to protect it."
